blogs.title: Finding My Place in the Kitchen
blogs.excerpt: From childhood memories around food to discovering my purpose as a plant-based Italian chef.
blogs.content:
Entering the culinary industry as someone who had never eaten meat came with a question I carried every day: "Is there a place for a chef like me here?"

During my early training years, my biggest challenge was navigating professional kitchens while staying connected to my values. I watched traditional techniques rely heavily on animal products, and there was a prevailing attitude that fine dining had to look a certain way.

There was a specific evening, during a grueling service, where I felt entirely disconnected from my purpose. I almost hung up my chef coat for good. But instead of leaving, I chose to change direction.

I realized my vegetarianism and compassion weren't limitations. They were my speciality. Leaving behind dairy, cheese, and traditional animal ingredients challenged me to understand food at a deeper level. I began exploring how to replicate the chemistry of classic Italian dishes using organic plant alternatives.

Today, I look back on that day of doubt as the catalyst for everything I build. The future of food belongs to innovation and compassion.

blogs.title: The Art of Vegan Cheese Crafting
blogs.excerpt: How understanding fermentation and traditional Italian dairy methods helped unlock rich, melting, plant-based mozzarella and aged parmesan.
blogs.content:
Cheese is the soul of Italian cuisine. When I set out to specialize in plant-based Italian cooking, the biggest challenge wasn't the pasta, but the cheese. How could you replicate the stretch of fresh mozzarella or the deep umami of aged Parmigiano-Reggiano without dairy?

Most commercial vegan cheeses rely on starches and oils. They melt, but they lack soul. I wanted to treat plant-based cheese-making like a traditional craft.

By leveraging raw cashews, almonds, and homemade rejuvelac (a fermented grain liquid), I began aging cheeses in controlled settings. The result was remarkable: we developed lactic acidity, complex rind structures, and a natural melt that rivals traditional cheeses.

In this blog, we explore the step-by-step fermentation science behind cashew-based mozzarella and how small-batch plant cheese can elevate restaurant menus.

blogs.title: Redefining Pasta: Keeping Tradition, Adding Compassion
blogs.excerpt: Perfecting handmade semolina dough, rich tomato reductions, and fresh herbal infusions without relying on eggs or dairy.
blogs.content:
Making pasta is a form of meditation. In traditional Italian cooking, egg yolks are often used to enrich fresh pasta dough, providing that signature bite and gold color.

However, by using high-quality Semola di Grano Duro (durum wheat semolina) and mapping the precise hydration levels, you can create a bite that is just as firm and toothsome—naturally egg-free.

In my kitchen, we combine slow-extruded and hand-rolled shapes with vibrant, slow-cooked sauces. A dash of saffron or carrot infusion can bring back that warm golden hue, while fresh basil oil provides a clean finish.

This article shares my recipe for the ultimate vegan fresh pasta dough and three simple sauces that let the durum wheat shine.

testimonials.text: Chef Akanksha redefined what we believed was possible with plant-based cuisine. Her attention to Italian traditions while introducing pure, cruelty-free ingredients left our guests in absolute awe.
testimonials.author: Chef Alessandro Rossi

testimonials.text: The training sessions she conducted for our university faculty were exceptional. Her deep understanding of vegan ingredients and structured cooking techniques inspired our entire department.
testimonials.author: Dr. Meera Sharma
testimonials.role: Head of Culinary Arts, Chandigarh University

testimonials.text: Akanksha's private dining experience was a masterclass in taste and compassion. Every course was beautifully presented, telling a story of handmade craftsmanship and plant-based innovation.
testimonials.author: Rohan Mehta

milestones.title: Faculty Vegan Culinary Workshop
milestones.description: Trained hotel management and culinary faculty in plant-based cooking techniques, vegan ingredients, and menu development.

milestones.title: Guest Chef Experience
milestones.description: Invited as a guest chef for a plant-based culinary event organized by Humane World for Animals, showcasing practical applications of compassionate and sustainable cuisine.

milestones.title: Plant-Based Training
milestones.description: Introduced teams to vegan cooking approaches, ingredient understanding and recipe adaptation.

milestones.title: Founder
milestones.description: Building my own vision for the future of plant-based dining.
